---
title: "OSC account setup"
subtitle: "Create an Ohio Supercomputer Center account if you don't have one"
date: 2026-07-01
date-modified: 2026-07-10
author:
  - name: Jelmer Poelstra
    orcid: 0000-0002-3514-7462
    email: poelstra.1@osu.edu
    affiliation: 
      - name: CFAES Bioinformatics Core, Ohio State University
        url: https://go.osu.edu/bioinformatics-core
number-sections: true
toc: true
---

---------

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

## Overview

During the workshop, we'll be using the Ohio Supercomputer Center (OSC) so we can all run code
and access data in the same manner.
Additionally, OSC's computing resources and data storage capabilities make it
the go-to location for us at OSU to perform real-world omics data analyses. 

The instructors will add you to the workshop's OSC project (`PAS3454`),
which will give you access to shared files and allow you to reserve computing resources.
If you don't yet have an OSC account, 
being added to the project will additionally trigger the process for you to create an account.

## What you should do

::: {.panel-tabset}

## If you already have an OSC account

1. Send us your OSC username (we can likely find it, but it's not foolproof and easier if you tell us).
   We will then add you to the OSC project, and you will receive an email informing you of this.

2. Check that you can log in at <https://ondemand.osc.edu> using your username and password.

3. Make sure you can access your password during the workshop:
   you'll need to enter it in the VS Code editor where your browser/computer password manager won't work.

## If you don't have an OSC account yet

1. We will add you to the OSC Project via your OSU email address,
   which will trigger an email from OSC.
   Follow the instructions in the email to create your account.

2. Check that you can log in at <https://ondemand.osc.edu> using your username and password
   (it may take up to half an hour after you create your account for this to work).

3. Make sure you can access your password during the workshop:
   you'll need to enter it in the VS Code editor where your browser/computer password manager won't work.
:::

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

-----

<hr style="height:1pt; visibility:hidden;" />

![This is the kind of email you will receive from OSC when you've been added to the workshop project and already have an account. If you don't have an account, you will receive a similar email but with a link to create one.](/img/osc-email.png){fig-align="center" width="70%" fig-alt="Screenshot of the OSC email inviting you to the workshop project"}
